title: Eucalyptus shower steam tablets
subtitle: Fizzing shower tablets with eucalyptus and peppermint for a steam-room effect
excerpt: Bicarbonate of soda and citric acid fizz when they contact water, releasing carbon dioxide. The essential oils go airborne in the steam. Place a tablet on the shower floor away from the direct water stream and the slow fizzing releases eucalyptus vapour into the shower for 10 to 15 minutes.
sourceNotes: Shower tablet formulation from DIY Natural (diynatural.com, May 2026). Citric acid and bicarbonate reaction ratios from cosmetic chemistry references. Eucalyptus and peppermint EO safety from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Work quickly once all ingredients are combined: the mixture will begin to fizz if exposed to moisture in the air. Work in a dry room on a dry day.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Press the mixture into moulds or roll into balls and leave to dry overnight. Once hardened, wrap individually in cling film or store in an airtight container.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months in an airtight container away from moisture. Humidity will cause premature fizzing and the tablets will crack and crumble. Wrap each tablet individually in cling film or foil if storing for more than a few days.

recipeTools.notes: Or any dry mixing bowl; do not use a damp bowl

glossaryTerms.definition: The release of carbon dioxide gas caused by the reaction between an acid (citric acid) and an alkali (bicarbonate of soda) in the presence of water. In shower tablets, the gentle water drips from the shower trigger this reaction gradually, releasing the essential oils with the CO2 gas into the steam.

content.text: The chemistry behind these tablets is the same as bath bombs: bicarbonate of soda (alkali) and citric acid fizz when they meet water. In the shower, the tablet sits away from the main stream and the light mist and condensation triggers slow
for 10 to 15 minutes. Keep the tablet away from the direct water stream: direct flow dissolves it in seconds. Place it near the drain or in a corner of the shower floor.

Eucalyptus and peppermint essential oils are toxic to cats, and peppermint should be kept well away from children under two years old. Both are strong mucous membrane stimulants: the vapour from the shower will be noticeably intense in an enclosed shower cubicle. Start with one tablet to gauge the intensity before using two.

items.prepNote: 2:1 ratio with bicarbonate; the acid that triggers the fizz
The primary vapour note; approximately 400 drops
Adds a cooling menthol layer to the steam

content.text: Combine the bicarbonate of soda and citric acid in a dry bowl. Mix thoroughly. In a separate small container, combine the two essential oils. Add the essential oil blend to the dry mixture one drop at a time, mixing continuously: adding too quickly can cause premature fizzing. When fully combined, the mixture should hold together when squeezed.

Press firmly into silicone moulds (round or square, approximately 30 g per tablet), or roll into balls by hand. Unmould immediately after pressing: do not leave in the mould or the tablets will stick. Alternatively, pack loosely into a container and scoop out by tablespoon for each use. Leave to harden at room temperature for 24 hours before wrapping or storing.
3 months in an airtight container, or individually wrapped in cling film. Do not store in a bathroom cabinet or any damp location: the tablets will begin fizzing prematurely. Use within 3 months.
